LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ROSS MIRKARIMI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION THREE, FIVE, AND SIX IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY PLEAD COMPLIANCE WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. (GOV. CODE SEC. 945.4.) THE DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. A PLAINTIFF CANNOT SUE UNDER THE UNRUH ACT FOR EMPLOYMENT DISCRIMINATION. (ROJO V. KLIGER (1990) 52 CAL.3D 65, 77.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION ("COA") FOR FEHA DISCRIMINATION, THE MOTION IS DENIED. AS TO THE 2ND COA FOR FEHA RETALIATION, THE MOTION IS DENIED. AS TO THE 3RD COA FOR LABOR CODE RETALIATION, THE MOTION IS DENIED. AS TO THE 5TH COA FOR IIED, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. AS TO THE 6TH COA FOR NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHILLIP A. TRUJILLO OF TRUJILLO & VINSON LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 746-7660 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA B. BERKOWITZ, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3825 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS SHERIFF ROSS MIRKARIMI AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. As to the 1st cause of action ("COA") for FEHA Discrimination, the motion is denied. Plaintiff raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether he suffered an adverse employment action. There is evidence that Plaintiff was given excessive workload and duties compared to female sergeants and that Plaintiff was assigned unnecessary, menial tasks. (Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts ("AMF") 44, 48, 50.) Liberally construing the evidence in favor of Plaintiff, a reasonable jury could find that this materially affected the terms of Plaintiff's employment. Defendant has not set forth a legitimate, nondiscriminatory reason for Plaintiff being given an excessive workload and being assigned menial tasks. As to the 2nd COA for FEHA Retaliation, the motion is denied. As set forth above, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff suffered an adverse employment action. P submits sufficient evidence to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether there is a causal link between the adverse employment actions and the filing of complaints and whether his supervisors knew about the complaints. (AMF 48,
50, 54.) As to the 3rd COA for Labor Code Retaliation, the motion is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff complained about violations of federal or state statutes, such as FEHA discrimination and harassment. Mueller v. County of Los Angeles (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 809 and Carter v. Escondido Union High School Dist. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 922, cited by Defendant, are distinguishable. As to the 4th COA for harassment, Plaintiff has submitted sufficient evidence to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether the conduct was severe and pervasive and that the conduct went beyond personnel management decisions. (AMF 14, 44, 47, 48, 68, 77, 90.) As to the 5th COA for IIED, the motion is Granted. As to the 6th COA for negligent supervision, the motion is granted. This cause of action is not based on a statute and is therefore barred as a matter of law. C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, cited by Plaintiff, is distinguishable. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with
CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendants
Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Continued To Mar-17-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearance on this matter. The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows:
DEFENDANTS SHERIFF ROSS MIRKARIMI AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The matter is continued to March 17, 2015 per the agreement of the parties. The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the reply brief until March 9, 2015, the day before the hearing. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ROSS MIRKARIMI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained. The demurrer to causes of action three, five, and six is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The complaint does not adequately plead compliance with the Government Claims Act. (Gov. Code sec. 945.4.) The demurrer to the seventh cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. A plaintiff cannot sue under the Unruh Act for employment discrimination. (Rojo v. Kliger (1990) 52 Cal.3d 65, 77.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
